title: Governance Session State
description: A session state captures the full governance context for an agent session, including the mission declaration, all observed events, and the latest reconciliation decision. Sessions progress through three phases: initialized (declaration loaded, no events yet), active (events streaming, reconciliation running), and closed (session terminated, final decision rendered).

id.description: Unique identifier for this governance session. Must be non-empty. All events, declarations, and decisions within the session reference this ID.

phase.description: Current lifecycle phase of the session. 'initialized' means the declaration is loaded but no events have been processed. 'active' means events are streaming and reconciliation is running. 'closed' means the session has been terminated and no further events will be accepted.

declaration.description: The mission declaration governing this session. Defines what the agent is permitted to do. May be absent only in error states or before initialization completes.

events.description: Chronologically ordered list of observed events in this session. May be empty during the initialized phase.

latest_decision.description: The most recent reconciliation decision for this session. Updated after each reconciliation pass. Absent before the first reconciliation completes.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this session was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent update to the session state. Updated on every event ingestion and reconciliation pass.
